overall_reasoning: The patch directly addresses the stated problem: OpenHarmony lacks `<source_location>`, and the existing workaround was both non-conforming (extending `std`) and unconditional in its include. The replacement uses correct feature detection, keeps the standard-library path for conforming toolchains, and preserves caller diagnostics via a single macro used by all existing call sites. Manifests are updated, the change is committed, and the trajectory demonstrates both build-path verification and a full successful core build. The only notable gap is the absence of a committed unit/regression test for the new public header, which is a weakness rather than a merge blocker given the simplicity of the shim and the compile/build verification performed.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: The header is short, constexpr-correct, and mirrors the standard accessors (line/column/file_name/function_name), so call sites read identically in both paths. Conditional structure is easy to follow. Lacks a brief doc comment explaining the dual-path intent, which keeps this from a perfect score, but naming and layout match surrounding code.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: Placement under `include/mbgl/util` matches existing util headers, the type name follows project CamelCase conventions, `SYM_GUARD_LOC` remains the single capture point so no call sites needed edits, and the header was added to both CMake and Bazel header lists exactly where alphabetical neighbors sit.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: The diff is confined to the new compatibility header, the four files that referenced `std::source_location` (symbol_instance.hpp/cpp, bucket.hpp, symbol_bucket.hpp/cpp), and the two build manifests that list public headers. No unrelated churn; a minor indentation slip introduced by the edit was corrected before commit.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: Removes the prior standards violation (user-defined type inside `namespace std`), guards the `<source_location>` include with `__has_include`, and introduces no runtime risk: the fallback stores only caller-provided string literals with constexpr accessors. Default-constructed values are safe empty/zero values.
rating_rationales.test_quality: No regression test was committed (e.g., a `test/util/source_location.test.cpp` exercising both paths would have been ideal for a public util header). Mitigating: the trajectory shows compile-time verification of the standard path, the C++17 fallback, and a simulated C++20-library-without-source_location path, plus a complete successful `mbgl-core` build under -Werror, so correctness of both branches is reasonably established.

strengths: Eliminates the pre-existing undefined behavior of injecting `std::source_location` into `namespace std`, replacing it with a project-owned `mbgl::util::SourceLocation`

Feature detection is robust: uses `__has_include` plus `__cpp_lib_source_location >= 201907L`, so OpenHarmony (no header), C++17, and partial C++20 libraries all fall back correctly while conforming C++20 toolchains use the real standard type
Preserves diagnostic usefulness: `SYM_GUARD_LOC`/`MBGL_CURRENT_SOURCE_LOCATION` still captures caller file, function, and line in both paths
Both relevant build manifests (CMakeLists.txt INCLUDE_FILES and bazel/core.bzl MLN_CORE_HEADERS) updated, and the change was committed with a clean worktree
Verification was thorough: isolated compile checks of the standard path, C++17 fallback path, and a forced missing-feature C++20 path, plus a full successful `mbgl-core` build with -Werror
summary: Adds `include/mbgl/util/source_location.hpp` providing `mbgl::util::SourceLocation`, aliasing `std::source_location` when genuinely available and otherwise falling back to a small constexpr class with matching accessors. All `std::source_location` uses in symbol guard code are migrated, the old non-conforming `namespace std` shim is removed, and CMake/Bazel manifests are updated. Scope is tight, the design handles the OpenHarmony case, and the change is committed.
